title: How to display a modal box with iframe when a button is clicked from "Card View"
excerpt:
Hello, 
I want to display a full-sized modal box with iframe inside when user clicks a button from my “card view” feature of application. Is it possible from monday js sdk? 
An example; 
  
  
Thanks!

posts.author: tgurler (Turan Gurler)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello,

I want to display a full-sized modal box with iframe inside when user clicks a button from my “card view” feature of application. Is it possible from monday js sdk?

An example;

Screen Shot 2021-06-04 at 19.05.483444×1326 264 KB

Screen Shot 2021-06-04 at 19.04.593486×2028 226 KB

Thanks!

posts.author: AlexSavchuk (Alex Savchuk)
posts.cookedHtml:
@tgurler

Thanks for reaching out. That’s a great question

This is definitely possible, although you will need to code the behavior in your app. This is not something that is built into to our SDK at this time.

I hope this helps confirm the options here.

-Alex

posts.author: dipro (Dipro Bhowmik)
posts.cookedHtml:
This is cool @AlexSavchuk! How did you learn this?

I always thought any UI from the item card would have to be constrained to the small pane on the right…

posts.author: Helen (Helen)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @tgurler!

Check out this recent post by our amazing community member @fatih for this functionality in our SDK.

We are working on getting this documented. Thanks!

posts.author: tgurler (Turan Gurler)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey, thanks for the replies. openAppFeatureModal has solved my problem. It’s better to have it on documentation.

Best,

Turan

posts.cookedHtml: This topic was automatically closed 7 days after the last reply. New replies are no longer allowed.
